steps.title: Identify the slope from the downward trend
steps.content:
The scatterplot shows a clear downward pattern: $y$ starts at $6$ when $x = 0$ and decreases to $1$ when $x = 5$.

Across $5$ units of $x$, $y$ drops by $5$, giving slope $-1$. Choices C and D have slope $6$ (positive), so they are eliminated.

steps.title: Determine the y-intercept to distinguish A and B
steps.content:
Both A and B have slope $-1$, so check the $y$-intercept. The leftmost point in the scatterplot is $(0, 6)$, so the $y$-intercept is $6$.

Choice A: $y = -x + 6$ has $y$-intercept $6$. Choice B: $y = -x - 6$ has $y$-intercept $-6$ (wrong).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
